layman_headline: Fear grips the crypto market — no quality setups justify risking capital right now.
layman_explanation: The overall crypto market is in a fearful state with Bitcoin down and altcoins bleeding even harder. Bitcoin dominance is high at 56%, meaning money is flowing out of smaller coins and into Bitcoin for safety. While a few coins have bounced today, none of the candidates show the clean technical setup — strong support, confirmed volume breakout, or bullish reversal pattern — needed to justify opening a new position. The best approach right now is patience.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market is still searching for a bottom, and chasing bounces in this environment is how traders get burned. Watch for fear to deepen further (below 20) or for BTC to hold a clear support level before deploying capital.
expert_reasoning: I evaluated all 9 candidates and none meet the bar for a high-conviction swing entry. KITE has a bearish marubozu and -1.40 chart bias — avoid. VELVET surged 27% in 24h but has a 0.26x R/R ratio and bearish chart bias — classic trap. LIT has RSI at 72 (overbought) after a 56% 5-day run — extreme correction risk. GRASS dropped 22% weekly but shows a bearish engulfing pattern and -0.90 chart bias — catching a falling knife. ETHFI has the best chart bias (+0.60) but only 2.5% upside to resistance with 0.11x R/R — terrible setup. JTO is the least bad option but still doesn't meet minimum criteria. BTC dominance at 56% creates a hostile environment for altcoin longs. Fear & Greed at 28 is improving but not at the extreme capitulation levels (sub-15) where contrarian buys historically pay off.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56% means alt positions face headwinds even if BTC stabilizes
Fear & Greed improving from 24.6 weekly avg to 28 could reverse — geopolitical tensions (Iran strikes) create binary event risk
All swing candidates lack volume breakout confirmation — the most reliable signal in our track record (100% win rate)
watch_list: Bitcoin ETF flow data over the next 48 hours — if inflows continue above $100M/day, that confirms the reversal in institutional sentiment
BTC price action at $62,000 support — a breakdown below would trigger another alt selloff
Fear & Greed index — if it drops below 20 while BTC holds support, that's historically been the best swing entry zone
market_context: The crypto market is in a fear regime with BTC at $62,775 and high dominance squeezing alts. Geopolitical tensions and stablecoin market cap shrinkage add caution, though Bitcoin ETF inflows snapping an 8-week outflow streak offer a glimmer of hope.
context_banner: Market fear is improving but not yet at capitulation levels — discipline says wait for a higher-quality setup with confirmed volume and clear support.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 28 (Fear) — improving from weekly avg of 24.6, but not at extreme capitulation levels where contrarian buys historically work best.
Bitcoin ETFs drew $197M snapping 8-week outflow streak — positive institutional signal but BTC price hasn't confirmed the move yet.
Best candidate JTO has RSI at 59 (neutral-high) while LIT is overbought at 72 — no oversold bounce setups available among candidates.
No candidate shows risk/reward above 2.0x — KITE has 6.0x R/R but deeply bearish chart (-1.40 bias). Every other candidate is below 1.3x.
Bitcoin holding near $63.8K despite war-driven selloff and nearing Fidelity's power law support line — macro backdrop is uncertain but crypto is showing relative resilience.
